title: cerastium pilosum f. amurense
intro: Cerastium pilosum f. amurense, also known as Amur mouse-ear chickweed, is a subspecies of the Cerastium pilosum plant, which belongs to the carnation family and is native to East Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems. The health benefits of Cerastium pilosum f. amurense include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reduce oxidative stress, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, and its leaves and stems can be dried or made into tinctures for medicinal purposes.
